name: Architext 1.7.5 Single Routing Model + npm Phase-Out + Self-Model Refresh
summary: Consolidation release. Routing is now exactly two routers and only two: one deterministic node-based MODEL for every box-and-line diagram (flows, system map, C4, deployment, dataflow, risk overlay) and the separate sequential router for sequence diagrams. The legacy candidate-based engine was physically removed (~13,300 LOC) — plan_diagram used to run it and then overwrite every route with the model, pure wasted work, which is why structural views spun for seconds; with it gone they render instantly and route geometry is byte-identical. npm continues to be phased out: the redundant per-platform npm dist co-location is dropped (the viewer is embedded in the binary — completing the item deferred from 1.7.4), and the npm 'root scripts' convenience feature plus the `migrate` command alias are removed. `init` joins `sync`/`install` as a synonym. The Architext self-model (this repo's own architecture data) was refreshed from JS-era source paths and npm verification to the real Rust crates. A wall-time guard now backs the corpus quality gate.
updateSource: Shipped 2026-06-22: npm latest = 1.7.5, GitHub release v1.7.5 published 2026-06-22T15:59:26Z. Routing engine removal verified output-preserving (27/27 corpus flow×view geometries byte-identical to pre-removal), §0 gate VALID (220 legal routes), workspace tests green, WASM viewer compiles against the slimmed routing crate. npm root-scripts + migrate removal verified (no rootScripts refs remain; migrate not a command; status reports no root scripts). init dispatches as sync. Self-model refresh validated with architext validate. Reviewed live on the FlowForge corpus.

required.title: One deterministic node-based router; legacy candidate engine removed
required.summary: Promote the deterministic model (route_all_coordinated) to the SOLE node-based router for all box-and-line diagrams (doublebacks and step formations costed out; facing-offset stem fix — no L flush to a surface, stems the only Z exception). Then physically delete the legacy candidate engine (~13,300 LOC: route_edges/orchestration, separation, construction; route_candidate_builders/ports; route_strategies; route_scoring; route_mount_model/). RCA: plan_diagram ran the engine and then apply_model_routes overwrote every route with the model's geometry — the engine's expensive search was computed and discarded on every diagram, which is why structural views (C4 / deployment / risk overlay / repo tree) spun for seconds in the browser (risk-overlay ~4000ms). With the engine gone they route in tens of ms (risk-overlay 44ms) and the rendered geometry is byte-identical (proven 27/27 corpus flow×view). The corpus quality gate was repointed onto the model with a regenerated, uniformly-cleaner baseline (all doglegs / pair-internal crossings / shared segments now 0).

required.title: Wall-time catastrophic-regression guard on the corpus gate
required.summary: The corpus_fitness gate now ratchets a wall-time bound (full-corpus model routing < 1000ms; real cost is a few ms, ~200x headroom) alongside the route-quality metrics, so a reintroduced super-linear routing pass (the kind the removed engine had) trips CI without flaking on slow runners.

required.title: Continue phasing out npm (dist co-location, root scripts, migrate alias)
required.summary: Drop the redundant per-platform npm dist co-location now that the viewer is embedded in the binary (~2.6 MB/package) — completes the drop-redundant-npm-co-location item deferred from 1.7.4. Remove the npm 'root scripts' convenience feature entirely (--root-scripts/--no-root-scripts flags, the ROOT_SCRIPTS aliases, the upsert logic, the status report, the sync prompt) and the `migrate` command alias (redundant with sync). Add a bare-binary embedded-serve CI smoke that boots with an empty dist so the off-npm embedded path is exercised. The copied-install migration ramp is intentionally KEPT until 2.0.0 (roadmap retire-copied-install-migration) so it doesn't strand pre-1.7.0 repos dirty.

required.title: `init` synonym for `sync`
required.summary: Add `init` as a synonym for `sync` (alongside `install`); all three dispatch identically. Recognized by the parser, dispatched in main, documented in usage/README.

required.title: Refresh the Architext self-model to the Rust-native codebase
required.summary: The project's own architecture data was JS-era stale: 11 of 18 nodes pointed sourcePaths at deleted viewer/src/**/*.js files, 0 referenced Rust crates, and ~10 verified via npm test — the 1.7.0 Rust rewrite was never reflected in the self-model. Refresh nodes/flows/decisions/risks from the real Rust crates (architext-cli / architext-core / architext-routing / architext-serve / architext-viewer): real sourcePaths, cargo/trunk/script verification, the routing node re-pointed to the model, npm phase-out reflected. Prose docs (README) updated for the same. Validated with architext validate.

required.title: Light/dark toggle reads as a swatch of the target theme
required.summary: The theme toggle now renders as a literal swatch of the theme it switches to (light button = light theme's surface/ink/line; dark button = dark theme's) with a single hued glyph — sun orange, moon purple — at the regular font size.

required.title: Restore PDF export (regressed out in the 1.7.0 rewrite)
required.summary: Browser-native PDF export was dropped in the 1.7.0 Rust/Leptos rewrite (no PDF control survived; the old test + UAT harness were deleted). Restored as a regression fix: a topbar 'PDF' control hands the active view to the browser print / save-as-PDF workflow (window.print, rAF-gated), and new @media print CSS strips the app chrome so the printed page is the diagram in the active theme's colors. Live-UI verified: the button fires window.print and the print stylesheet hides nav/inspector/topbar/controls.

workstreams.name: Single node-based routing model
workstreams.summary: Make the deterministic model the sole node-based router, delete the legacy engine, and guard the corpus gate on quality + wall time.

workstreams.name: npm phase-out
workstreams.summary: Drop redundant npm dist co-location, remove the npm root-scripts feature and the migrate alias, and add a bare-binary serve smoke.

workstreams.name: CLI + viewer ergonomics
workstreams.summary: init synonym for sync; theme-toggle restyle.

workstreams.name: Documentation currency
workstreams.summary: Refresh the Architext self-model to the Rust crates and update prose docs.

evidence.label: Engine removal byte-identical: 27/27 corpus flow×view geometries unchanged from a clean relink
corpus_fitness repointed onto the model with regenerated baseline + wall-time guard (cargo test -p architext-routing --test corpus_fitness)
§0 forbidden-artifact gate VALID — 220 legal routes (audit_model on the corpus)
PDF export restored + live-UI verified (topbar PDF control fires window.print; @media print strips chrome)
